name: BlackHole
description:
BlackHole — Kerr black hole with relativistic geodesic lensing, accretion-disk shading, and orbiting particle volumetric density. Migrated from legacy black_hole.rs onto node.wgsl_compute (the naga-introspected dynamic escape hatch) + existing primitive vocabulary (seed_particles / resolve_accumulator / gaussian_blur). 19 nodes: 4 wgsl_compute (deflection bake, Schwarzschild orbit integrator, polar+hemisphere splat, cinematic display compositor), 1 seed_particles allocator, 2 resolve_accumulator, 10 gaussian_blur (deflection H/V × 3 + polar density H/V × 2), and 3 conversion nodes (tilt/rotate deg→rad via affine_scalar, scale→uv_scale via math.Reciprocal).

Particles self-seed on frame 1 via simulate's life<0.5 respawn path (seed_particles ships all 800k slots dead via active_count=0). The legacy 'seed' entry point is dropped — its only behavioural difference (staggered ages) is cosmetic since BlackHole has no age-based death. Frame-counter for spawn randomization computed inline as u32(time_val*60) since system.generator_input has no per-frame counter.

Approximate-parity migration — not yet visually verified against legacy on the canonical fixture. Known parity deltas:
(1) Gaussian blur uses node.gaussian_blur fixed 17-tap kernel; legacy uses custom σ=4 21-tap. Visually comparable.
(2) Deflection bake runs every frame; legacy rebakes only on camera/scale/spin param change. Adds ~150-step ray trace per pixel per frame to the cost.
(3) Deflection runs at full canvas resolution; legacy runs at quarter-res. ~16× per-pixel cost until output_canvas_scale lands on the dynamic node.
(4) Particle respawn position varies with u32(time*60) (legacy: per-instance u64 frame_count).
(5) Disk shear over time uses display.orbit_angle = 0 (legacy: time*speed*0.3). Disk doesn't rotate over time. Add an LFO + math chain in a follow-up.
